channel.title: Home on Peter Gao
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Peter Gao

item.title: Playing with d3 and blogdown
item.description: .bar { fill: steelblue; } .bar:hover { fill: orange; } library("jsonlite") cat( paste( '<script> var data = ',toJSON(cars),'; </script>' , sep="") ) var data = [{"speed":4,"dist":2},{"speed":4,"dist":10},{"speed":7,"dist":4},{"speed":7,"dist":22},{"speed":8,"dist":16},{"speed":9,"dist":10},{"speed":10,"dist":18},{"speed":10,"dist":26},{"speed":10,"dist":34},{"speed":11,"dist":17},{"speed":11,"dist":28},{"speed":12,"dist":14},{"speed":12,"dist":20},{"speed":12,"dist":24},{"speed":12,"dist":28},{"speed":13,"dist":26},{"speed":13,"dist":34},{"speed":13,"dist":34},{"speed":13,"dist":46},{"speed":14,"dist":26},{"speed":14,"dist":36},{"speed":14,"dist":60},{"speed":14,"dist":80},{"speed":15,"dist":20},{"speed":15,"dist":26},{"speed":15,"dist":54},{"speed":16,"dist":32},{"speed":16,"dist":40},{"speed":17,"dist":32},{"speed":17,"dist":40},{"speed":17,"dist":50},{"speed":18,"dist":42},{"speed":18,"dist":56},{"speed":18,"dist":76},{"speed":18,"dist":84},{"speed":19,"dist":36},{"speed":19,"dist":46},{"speed":19,"dist":68},{"speed":20,"dist":32},{"speed":20,"dist":48},{"speed":20,"dist":52},{"speed":20,"dist":56},{"speed":20,"dist":64},{"speed":22,"dist":66},{"speed":23,"dist":54},{"speed":24,"dist":70},{"speed":24,"dist":92},{"speed":24,"dist":93},{"speed":24,"dist":120},{"speed":25,"dist":85}]; experimenting with d3 (from http://bl.ocks.org/alexsb/8565055) var data = [150, 230, 180, 90]; var svg = d3.select("body") .append("svg") .attr("width", 300) .attr("height", 200); svg.selectAll(".bar") .data(data) .enter() .append("rect") .attr({ class : "bar", width : function(d) {return d;}, height: "40", y : function(d, i) {return i*50 + 10;}, x : "10" }); var data = [150, 230, 180, 90]; var svg = d3.

item.title: About Me
item.description: I am currently pursuing a PhD in Statistics at the University of Washington. My research interests center upon the uses of spatial and spatiotemporal statistics in environmental science and the social sciences. I am currently researching statistical postprocessing methods for improving forecasts for sea ice thickness with Adrian Raftery and Cecilia Bitz. I graduated from the University of Chicago in 2017 with an MS in Statistics and a BS in Mathematics and Statistics.

item.title: Other Links
item.description: This school year, John Best and I are organizing the Space/Time Reading Group on spatiotemporal statistics and related topics.

item.title: Space-Time Reading Group
item.description: The Space-Time Reading Group meets weekly to discuss current research in spatiotemporal statistics and related fields. In Autumn 2018, we are reading and discussing Advanced Spatial Modeling with Stochastic Partial Differential Equations Using R and INLA by Elias T. Krainski, Virgilio Gómez-Rubio, Haakon Bakka, Amanda Lenzi, Daniela Castro-Camilo, Daniel Simpson, Finn Lindgren and Håvard Rue (website and Gitbook). Meetings are on Tuesdays from 12:30 - 1:30pm in the CSSS conference room (Padelford C14A).

item.title: Teaching
item.description: at the University of Washington: Teaching Assistant, STAT 394 Probability I (Winter 2020) Teaching Assistant, STAT 220 Principles of Statistical Reasoning (Spring 2018) Teaching Assistant, STAT 221 Statistical Concepts and Methods for the Social Sciences (Winter 2018) Teaching Assistant, STAT 221 Statistical Concepts and Methods for the Social Sciences (Fall 2017) In Summer 2019, I taught Applied Math and Logical Reasoning to middle school students as Junior Faculty at BEAM Discovery,
